answer: Hungarian forms locatives by adding suffixes -ba/-be (into) or -ra/-re (onto) to place names. The choice depends on vowel harmony: back vowels take -ba/-ra, front vowels take -be/-re. Words ending in 'a' often change to 'á' before the suffix.
